text.title: Motion and Micro-Interaction System Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Motion and Micro-Interaction System application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Four durations: micro (100ms), small (200ms), medium (300ms), large (500ms). Easings: ease-out (entry), ease-in (exit), ease-in-out (move). No ad-hoc values.
rule.rationale: Material + iOS + Fluent converged on 4-duration scale; ad-hoc values create perceived inconsistency.

rule.statement: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) applied to ALL motion; reduces or removes. Test with macOS Reduce Motion ON.
rule.rationale: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.3.3; legally required; affects ~5-10% of users.

rule.statement: Motion is used as perceived-performance tool: optimistic UI, skeleton, progress. Wait states ≥500ms get explicit progress UI.
rule.rationale: Doherty threshold (400ms) + Nielsen's response-time guidelines: 500ms is the perception cliff.

rule.statement: Multi-element transitions follow choreography: stagger ≤50ms between siblings; parent moves before children; exit before enter.
rule.rationale: Choreography prevents 'jumbled' transitions that read as broken; explicit rules create visual order.

rule.statement: No decorative looping animations on idle screens. Distracting + a11y issue (vestibular disorders).
rule.rationale: Looping decoration creates cognitive load + accessibility complaints; restrict to load/transition states.
